preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar lateral fricative [ɮ] to the voiceless [ɬ] is a form of devoicing. It often occurs word-finally or before voiceless sounds as part of a general devoicing trend in the language's obstruent system.

examples.note: Devoicing of lateral fricatives in specific phonotactic environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
